headline: Dollar General: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: The results exceeded Wall Street expectations. The average estimate of 26 analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $1.61 per share. The discount retailer posted revenue of $10.91 billion in the period, also topping Street forecasts.

content.title: Dollar General: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
content.summary: The results exceeded Wall Street expectations. The average estimate of 26 analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $1.61 per share. The discount retailer posted revenue of $10.91 billion in the period, also topping Street forecasts.

headline: Dollar General (NYSE:DG) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4 CY2025
summary: Discount retailer Dollar General (NYSE:DG) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q4 CY2025, with sales up 5.9% year on year to $10.91 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.93 per share was 17.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Dollar General Q4 net income jumps 122.9%, sees modest 2026 sales growth
summary: Net income climbed to $426.3m while diluted earnings per share surged 121.8% to $1.93, compared with $0.87 in the prior-year period.

headline: Dollar General Is Down 7% — Here’s Why Wall Street Is Divided on DG Stock

headline: Dollar General Stock Falls Even as Earnings Surprise. Here’s Why.
summary: Shares in  Dollar General  tumbled Thursday even after the discount retailer posted quarterly earnings and sales that beat consensus expectations.  Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for profit of $1.66 a share on sales of $10.8 billion.  Comparable sales, or those from stores and digital channels in operation for at least a year, rose 4.3%.

content.title: Dollar General Stock Falls Even as Earnings Surprise. Here’s Why.
content.summary: Shares in  Dollar General  tumbled Thursday even after the discount retailer posted quarterly earnings and sales that beat consensus expectations.  Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for profit of $1.66 a share on sales of $10.8 billion.  Comparable sales, or those from stores and digital channels in operation for at least a year, rose 4.3%.
